metrics.narrative: Pain is vivid but never quantified. On the 29 Oct 2025 intro, Jonas Elber described purchase orders 'submitted as free text, leading to misalignment between buyer descriptions and available products and services' and a procurement process across 'too many platforms, leading to inefficiencies and a purely manual workflow.' On 20 Nov 2025 he framed the spend-control problem: 'people are very creative when it comes to placing new orders... it's difficult for me as responsible for the spend to see what we are spending our money on.' No cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure was ever stated, and the Salesforce metrics fields (Value_to_Customer__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c) are all blank. Quantifying the cost of free-text/maverick spend and the AP manual-matching effort is the move to harden the business case the champion is now writing.

champion.narrative: Jonas Elber is a strong and strengthening champion. He is the SF Decision Maker contact role (Head of Technology), drove the agenda across all demos, pre-aligned the team before each session ('I really like to align with people before we go in there'), declared himself a decision-taker on CLM, and is now - per Next_Step_History 29 May 2026 - 'promoted to Head of Technology at B&O' and 'preparing the business case' with the new proposal in hand. On 25 Nov 2025 he endorsed Zycus's AI as ahead of the competition. What is still developing: his advocacy has not yet pulled the CFO/finance economic buyer or procurement leadership (Henrik Jensby, Folkert Bolger) into the room, and the deal remains single-threaded through him. His promotion increases his internal weight, which is the lever to convert into multi-threading and a board introduction.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and owned. P2P: uncontrolled free-text/maverick spend in the custom 'Bear Shop' app on SAP ECC, which Jonas called 'error-prone', lacking dual-ERP support, and slated for decommission next year; spend visibility and reporting are poor. CLM: ~10,000 contracts stored in SharePoint that are 'difficult to manage', with no common, standardized approval process (Christine: 'we each do it differently'). Process misalignment between procurement (owns sourcing) and finance (owns P2P) is a root cause Jonas named on 20 Nov 2025. Pain owners are Jonas (procurement/technology) and finance (P2P system owner). Confirmed pain across multiple calls; the inferred extension is the change-management risk Jonas himself flagged around legacy user behaviour ('especially with Christine').

decision_process.narrative: The process is partly mapped from the call sequence and history. Two parallel projects (CLM and P2P) run through one buying group led by Jonas Elber, recently promoted to Head of Technology. On 25 Nov 2025 Jonas said 'I don't think we will do an RFP... I'm already involved in it now and I'm one of who takes the decision on the contract lifecycle benefit'; the P2P decision sits with the finance system owner. Both projects then route to a CFO-chaired IT and digital board for final approval. Next_Step_History tracks the funnel: SteerCo met (Jan-Feb 2026), CLM vendor selected and P2P down-selected to 3 vendors (13 Apr), new proposal delivered (08 May), champion preparing the business case (29 May). The gap is the timeline and gate dates into the board, and finance engagement, both currently stalled by year-end workload.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well-evidenced from the demo series. The buyer is evaluating: AI-driven capability (Jonas on 25 Nov 2025 praised that Zycus has 'come quite far' on AI 'compared to competition'), dual-ERP support across SAP ECC and a likely Microsoft Dynamics future (raised repeatedly 20-21 Nov), elimination of free-text/maverick spend via catalog adoption, CLM migration of ~10,000 SharePoint contracts with AI metadata extraction, Outlook/Word integration for legal (Christine's requirement), automated 3-way invoice matching, and data privacy (Jonas pressed hard on multi-LLM orchestration and that learning stays 'solely from your platform'). Modular, unbundled pricing is an explicit criterion: Jonas insisted 'I don't want you to make a bundle because these are two different projects.' These are confirmed buyer-stated requirements across four transcribed demos.

competitors.name: Unknown SharePoint-layer CLM vendor
competitors.quote: I can be very transparent with you, we are looking into a solution that sits on top of SharePoint today.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus CLM as a purpose-built AI-native platform (contract risk monitor, Merlin negotiation agent, InstaReview) vs. a SharePoint overlay, and anchor to Jonas's stated pain: 10,000 contracts in SharePoint are already difficult to manage, so a layer on top of the same broken foundation will not solve the problem. Prove the AI differentiation with a live contract-discovery POC on B&O's SharePoint repository.

competitors.name: SAP ECC Bear Shop (incumbent, being decommissioned)
competitors.quote: SAP ECC is a really old platform, and we have developed an application on top of this which we call Bear Shop... it has a lot of errors, a lot of limitations, and it cannot support dual ERPs. This application will be decommissioned during next year.
competitors.how_we_win: Incumbent is already defeated (being decommissioned). No active threat.

competitors.name: Unknown SharePoint-native CLM vendor
competitors.quote: We are looking into a solution that sits on top of SharePoint today (Jonas Elber, 21 Nov 2025 CLM demo)
competitors.source: Avoma CLM demo 21 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus AI advantage (InstaReview, Negogenie, Merlin metadata extraction, contract risk monitor) + Word integration (WordConnect plugin, validated by Christine 21 Nov) + proven bulk SharePoint migration (AI-powered, demoed 21 Nov). Position Zycus as the AI-first CLM that enhances SharePoint rather than replacing it, with faster time-to-value than a native SharePoint layer. Surface the competitor name and feature set, then run a head-to-head AI capability demo.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics P2P (potential)
competitors.quote: We are a small company to run two large ERPs. I would be surprised if we don't go the Microsoft way, but there needs to be a decision (Jonas Elber, 20 Nov 2025)
competitors.source: Avoma alignment call 20 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: Dual-ERP support (SAP ECC + Dynamics) is Zycus's differentiator, demoed 21 and 27 Nov 2025. Position Zycus as ERP-agnostic, allowing B&O to run both ERPs in parallel during transition without P2P lock-in. If B&O moves to Dynamics, Zycus integrates; if they stay SAP, Zycus integrates. Microsoft Dynamics P2P locks B&O into Microsoft's roadmap and may not support SAP ECC well. Make the Merlin AI invoice processing (AP Smartdesk, 3-way matching, auto-extraction) the Phase 2 centerpiece, as Microsoft's AI for P2P is less mature.

competitors.name: Bear Shop (SAP ECC custom app, incumbent P2P)
competitors.quote: Bear Shop has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions. This application will be decommissioned during next year (Jonas Elber, 20 Nov 2025)
competitors.source: Avoma alignment call 20 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: Bear Shop is explicitly being decommissioned and is not a competitive threat. It is the pain Zycus is solving. Use Bear Shop's limitations (errors, no dual-ERP, free-text reliance, poor spend visibility) as the baseline pain in every commercial and board presentation.

competitors.name: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository)
competitors.quote: 10,000 contracts stored in SharePoint, difficult to manage (Jonas Elber, 21 Nov 2025 CLM demo)
competitors.source: Avoma CLM demo 21 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
competitors.how_we_win: SharePoint is the pain Zycus is solving for CLM. It has no lifecycle management, no AI, no approval workflows, no risk monitoring. Zycus's bulk SharePoint migration with AI metadata extraction (demoed 21 Nov) allows B&O to keep SharePoint as the storage layer while Zycus adds the intelligence and process layer on top. This is a 'both/and' positioning, not a rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: Two unnamed shortlisted vendors (P2P final field)
competitors.quote: P2P approved. Zycus Shortlisted between 3 vendors. Anthony to align with Jonas around pricing.
competitors.how_we_win: Identify the two rivals via Jonas, then anchor on AI/Merlin invoice intake and dual-ERP handling where they are weakest.

competitors.name: SAP ECC 'Bear Shop' (incumbent custom P2P app)
competitors.quote: It is an application that has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions. So this application will be decommissioned during next year.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the modern dual-ERP replacement that removes free-text spend; incumbent is already being retired.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics (future ERP direction)
competitors.quote: I would be surprised if we don't go Microsoft way, but of course there needs to be a decision on this.
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize Zycus handles multiple ERPs natively, so the Dynamics path does not require re-platforming procurement.

P2P scope approved, QIT submitted, 2 demos completed (Nov 2025), commercial discussions and pricing alignment ongoing through Apr 2026. Same Jonas-led B&O buying group as the parallel CLM deal (006P700000P69IM), targeting May 2026 implementation. • Capped below 8 because BD_Engagement is 'Unengaged', no upcoming meetings logged, and stage slip and close-date push flags are active. Score 7 reflects buyer-stated 'need AI for invoice extractions and Intake' but operational late-stage friction.

value.name: Microsoft Dynamics P2P (potential)
value.quote: We are a small company to run two large ERPs. I would be surprised if we don't go the Microsoft way, but there needs to be a decision (Jonas Elber, 20 Nov 2025)
value.source: Avoma alignment call 20 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
value.how_we_win: Dual-ERP support (SAP ECC + Dynamics) is Zycus's differentiator, demoed 21 and 27 Nov 2025. Position Zycus as ERP-agnostic, allowing B&O to run both ERPs in parallel during transition without P2P lock-in. If B&O moves to Dynamics, Zycus integrates; if they stay SAP, Zycus integrates. Microsoft Dynamics P2P locks B&O into Microsoft's roadmap and may not support SAP ECC well. Make the Merlin AI invoice processing (AP Smartdesk, 3-way matching, auto-extraction) the Phase 2 centerpiece, as Microsoft's AI for P2P is less mature.

packets.subject: Microsoft Dynamics P2P (potential)

value.name: Bear Shop (SAP ECC custom app, incumbent P2P)
value.quote: Bear Shop has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions. This application will be decommissioned during next year (Jonas Elber, 20 Nov 2025)
value.source: Avoma alignment call 20 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
value.how_we_win: Bear Shop is explicitly being decommissioned and is not a competitive threat. It is the pain Zycus is solving. Use Bear Shop's limitations (errors, no dual-ERP, free-text reliance, poor spend visibility) as the baseline pain in every commercial and board presentation.

packets.subject: Bear Shop (SAP ECC custom app, incumbent P2P)

value.name: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository)
value.quote: 10,000 contracts stored in SharePoint, difficult to manage (Jonas Elber, 21 Nov 2025 CLM demo)
value.source: Avoma CLM demo 21 Nov 2025, Jonas Elber
value.how_we_win: SharePoint is the pain Zycus is solving for CLM. It has no lifecycle management, no AI, no approval workflows, no risk monitoring. Zycus's bulk SharePoint migration with AI metadata extraction (demoed 21 Nov) allows B&O to keep SharePoint as the storage layer while Zycus adds the intelligence and process layer on top. This is a 'both/and' positioning, not a rip-and-replace.

packets.subject: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository)
